title: March 2024 Core Update & Spam Updates with Lily Ray

content.text: hey there happy Friday uh Happy core
update and spam update week I just got
back to New York from Las Vegas I was at
the pubcon conference which was pretty
fun and a bit surreal to be surrounded
by a bunch of old school seos while one
of the biggest updates in Google's
history was rolling out so that was a
very fun week and to add to that I was
presenting specifically about this topic
so we got the announcement that the
update was happening on March 5th and I
presented on March 6th so I had to go
update all my slides which I will share
the link to the slides as well but that
was fun never a dull moment in SEO
however today I wanted to talk about the
March 4 update and the March spam update
so really the March updates there are
multiple concurrent updates and
developments happening at the same time
so there's a lot of people talking about
this already including me I've been
doing my best to chime in Via social
media over the last few days all
although I really want to make a video
because there's a lot to be expected
there's a lot of misunderstandings
misconceptions um a lot of nuance and uh
myself and I know many others have been
reading between the lines really really
carefully to try to understand what's
going on and what Google's communicating
and how things are going to work and how
things are not working and all that so
this continues to roll out there's kind
of new discussions and developments
happening all the time but I want to
share what I know so far and so we'll
start by talking about the fact that the
March update was not just a core update
and it's not just a Spam update it's two
things happening at once why is that
important um because core updates which
this appears like it's going to be a
very big one that's its own beast that's
its own animal there there's core
updates usually about three to four
times every year could be a little bit
more or less um but that's nothing new
that's been something that Google's been
rolling out throughout its history and I
talk and write a lot about those if you
ever want to read what happened during a
court update I usually have one article
per Court update um I'll link to that as
well but with this one what's
interesting is uh according to John
Mueller on LinkedIn he mentioned that uh
this was a really big one they've been
working really really hard on this one
and it's going to take a while to roll
out and he actually specifically said a
month so what's tricky about core update
rollouts is that they take a while to
get a really good and consistent
understanding of how it's impacting your
site so you can sometimes see really
early signs like especially if you use a
tool like CX or SE samr or HRS of course
your own data but even search console
has a two-day delay so that's not always
the best place to look but you know if
you have your own ranking tools or
Google analytics you can kind of see
pretty early on if there's a big change
in in traffic or any kind of anomaly
there but uh you really do have to wait
a couple of weeks so usually a core
update takes two to three weeks to roll
out this one's probably going to be four
maybe more so wait until if you can wait
until Google's done rolling it out to
draw conclusions I know that's really
difficult myself I'll probably have
written an article by that point because
four weeks is just too long to wait but
uh we will have early signs before then
but do you know don't lose hope if
you're seeing any type of big anomaly or
traffic change in the first few days
because you'll often see Google like
reverse course throughout the course of
an update and I think that's why their
needs so long with this one because they
really want to make sure that they got
it right before they finish rolling it
out so that was announced on March 5th
today's March 8th so three days ago
we'll just announce to that I would say
today is the first day that we're
starting to see some pretty clear
patterns in in the third party tools
like districts and everything where you
can start to see some changes in
visibility um even just as late as last
night I was looking in search console
and I wasn't really seeing any clear
data rolling yet so be patient um that
will take a little bit of time to start
seeing how that's impacting your sites
if at all now that's the core update
everything I just talked about is the
core updates not the same as bam updates
so let's talk about Google new spam
update that in fact also did start
rolling out on Tuesday so three days ago
and that comes with a new batch of
manual actions being sent out by
Google's web spam team for anybody who's
unfamiliar Google's webpam team is the
one that issues manual actions also
known as penalties to websites that have
been violating Google spam guidelines
there's a whole slew of different types
of spam guidelines and reasons that you
can sent manual actions also nothing new
this has been the case for 25 years of
Google's existence but what's different
now is that there's new types of spam
policy violations that Google has
introduced there's actually three new
types so Google's uh site reputation
abuse which we'll talk about in a little
bit is a new type of spam update there's
scaled content abuse that's I think the
newest and most interesting one because
that's the one that really gets into AI
content which has been the big question
Mark in the SEO industry for the last
year how is Google going to treat that
we'll talk about that um and the last
one is expired domain abuse which
basically just means if you have been
buying expired domains repurposing those
domains with new content that has
nothing to do with what the domain used
to be about that's technically a form of
spam that's also something that seos
have been doing for forever so and you
know seos like Mike King will tell you
he just did a wonderful keynote about
how Google's actually told us in the
past there's no problem with that and
now they're telling us it is a problem
so this is one of the reasons why people
like me are so incredibly risk averse I
don't think a lot of people understand
my SEO history started when the website
that I worked for got hit by the penguin
update this was in 2012 and I was
responsible granted my boss was
encouraging me to buy link so we were
both responsible but it was working
really well we were buying links all
over Black Hat World it worked really
really well and then I woke up one
morning and the Penguin penalty or sorry
penguin update wiped out all of our SEO
traction for years so there is a reason
I am this way this is not because I'm a
Google suck up this is because I'm an
SEO professional and I want the sites
that I work on to succeed I want my
clients to succeed this is why I'm
always thinking about the most risk
averse way to approach SEO because I
hate seeing websites that are legitimate
businesses who are trying to do things
right get affected by Google updates but
as we can all see by now it happens all
the time because people don't understand
what Google's really
suggesting to do or not do in many cases
but if you read between the lines that
the patterns are very very clear so my
team and I have been working for years
helping websites recover from core
updates penalties manual actions all of
this so what I share is what I've
learned during that Journey anyway the
spam updates that started getting sent
out this week Google's using what's
called a pure spam update for many of
these types of sites this can be applied
to your entire domain it can be applied
applied to a subfolder or a sub doain
and this has been happening in fact just
that has been happening this week
There's Been many sites that have been
hit on the domain level so if you do a
site colon search for their site the
whole thing is removed from search the
rankings are removed from search if you
Google the name of their company their
brand name you don't see the website
appearing in top results now there's
already list circulating about which
sites these were I would say by and
large maybe 80 to 90% of them leaving
room for exceptions but
the vast majority of them were using
some type of scaled AI content
generation or
automation I am not saying AI content is
bad never said that I'm have been saying
since day one that you need to be really
careful why because Google's guidance
specifically says and has said since day
one that using automation to create
content at scale without little human
oversight is against its guidelines what
makes it really tricky is they also told
us last year that AI content in and of
itself is okay it just has to follow
those
guidelines uh I thought Google should
have done a more clear job of explaining
that the types of SEO and content
approaches that a lot of people have
been using for the last year are
actually a violation but it's true that
they've had that there the whole time
and a lot of people just Cho chose to
ignore it and at the same time I totally
understand that for the past year doing
automation with a lot of these AI
generation tools has worked quite well
so like all things in SEO it works until
it doesn't just because something is
working for a short period of time does
not mean it will work forever and you
can't be surprised if and when Google
chooses to take that traffic away from
you especially if you've been doing
something that falls into the gray area
of what they advise to not do in their
spam and content quality guidelines so
that's what's happening this is the
Reckoning for many different people that
have been Mass autogenerating content
without little oversight generally
speaking those are the types of peer
spam penalties that we're seeing issued
this week now I know there's exceptions
I've been talking to some people
individually Google is also going after
websites that are affiliated with those
types of sites and yes this does indeed
seem like it it is personal in some
cases not all cases but the people who
have been flag flagrantly sharing their
SEO approaches and content approaches
and listen I'm not casting any judgment
on these people they can do whatever
they
want that's just a really good way to
get caught so there seems to be a little
bit of that happening here
Google seems to be looking at those
examples and not only the sites that
they have but the sites that they're
affiliated with and guess what Google's
smarter than us they have more data than
us they can do this in really
sophisticated ways although it's really
not that hard you know looking at
somebody's the history of sites that
they've worked on or what they've posted
in forums or what link what sites they
link to and things like this it's really
not that complicated for even a human to
figure out which is What's Happening
Here is Google's human webpam team doing
this so yeah this is yet another reason
why I choose to be really proactive with
not getting sites in trouble because
Google is on to us they're more onto us
than we might realize so pure spam
manual actions have been sent out
thousands of them there's already a list
being shared about the types of sites
that these are you can look at them I
would say generally speaking they
deserved it sorry but like these sites
are not good for the internet guys like
these are not good sites for users on
Google and search engines to be seeing
in the results I I understand you make
money it's great but like objectively
people don't want to be reading this
spammy content you can tell yourself
it's great but like it's just not I'm
sorry again I know there's exceptions
but like let's just be a little bit more
honest with ourselves because tricking
yourself into believing that something's
helpful when all the signs indicate that
it's not it's just it's it's a better
approach to say you know what they got
me that didn't work let's figure out
what does work and let's let's do that
and guess what what does work is content
that's helpful for real human beings
Google is pretty damn good at figuring
that out by now so that's what's
happening there's going to be new manual
actions there's going to be Google
sending out new types of manual actions
I know like Glen Gabe for example shared
that he's seeing some sites getting
misleading content manual actions uh
we've had clients get those over the
years if you do something like say um
the release date for this new Netflix
series uh is going to be coming up you
know see when see when it is right and
then you go to the article and it does
not have the release date or it says we
don't know when the release date is
that's the type of thing you can get a
manual action for for misleading content
it's essentially just clickbait so if
you're using clickbaity headlines which
something that we do a lot for Google
discover what you're promising in the
headline in the article needs to be
somewhere in there and ideally at the
top of the page for the user to be to be
able to find it easily if you're doing
too much of that at scale you can get a
misleading uh misleading content manual
action and I anticipate we'll see new
and different types of manual actions
being sent out over the coming weeks
just because it hasn't affected your
site yet doesn't mean you're in the
clear it could happen so check your
search console uh security and manual
actions report go in there deliberately
and click manual actions because they're
not always going to send an email or it
could be like attached to some super old
email address that you have or something
so if you care about your sites go check
those one by one and see if there's any
type of warning
there uh the next part of the update is
site reputation abuse also known uh
lovingly by the SEO industry as parasite
SEO so Google's been a a little bit
vague about which sites fall into this
category and I know a lot of people for
the last six months have been kind of
like debating whether or not certain
types of websites and businesses fit
this criteria but essentially what
they're saying is that if you have a
portion of your website that's being
used for thirdparty Content often like
sponsored content that doesn't have much
to do with the main site the whole site
can be effective so the host parent
website can be affected um I know a lot
of people think this is going to mean
Forge this is going to mean Cena this is
going to mean CNN like a lot of big
players on the internet have been doing
something along these lines that's no
secret because because they get to rank
in really prominent positions I don't
personally know how much like the extent
to which Google's going to roll this out
to the bigger sites I really don't know
all of us are sitting here waiting to
find out how aggressive they're going to
be what makes this very very interesting
is that they're giving a two-month grace
period so on March 5th they told us that
you have two months to clean up your act
you have until May 5th for this portion
of the ranking roll out ranking system
or ranking algorithm update to roll out
so that's a interesting development a
lot of us are wondering like what's up
with the warning uh does Google need
people to fess up does Google want to
see how people respond to that you know
will the big sites decide to de-index a
lot of that content I think these are
all really interesting questions but I
don't remember Google ever issu issuing
any type of two-month warning before
wouldn't that be nice if we had that for
other algorithm updates um so that's the
lay of the land right now and some
advice I guess so if you are in a pure
spam penalty environment situation
you've had that you know sent to your
site your site has been removed from
search a portion of your site has been
removed from search uh you've been
caught red-handed okay so trying to do
something tricky and like maybe Google
won't notice if I do this or if I move
the content over here like it's just not
a good use of your time if you want to
salvage your site or your content in
that situation you need to fess up you
need to take ownership of what happened
you need to tell Google's webpam team
what you did because they already know
they're waiting to see if you're going
to admit it okay so if you're like oh
that wasn't me I didn't use AI content I
know a lot of people just don't believe
that Google can see that it's AI content
but listen I can look at it for five
seconds and I know it's AI content I
think most people that use AI tools can
do the same relatively easily and even
if it's not 100% AI generated if it
looks and feels like it's 100% AI
generated you should still take
ownership of the fact that Google
considers that content to be spammy just
because you think it looks great does
not mean that other people do so take
ownership remove yourself from any kind
of sketchy situation and again this is
all assuming that you want to save this
website if you don't care about the site
it's fine buy you can let it go but if
you want to save the website take
ownership explain what happened remove
the offending content from your website
ideally figure out a way to get back in
Google's good graces in terms of your
content strategy if you're going to
salvage this website what are you going
to do going forward to make it a
trustworthy website because you've
already been caught red-handed people
love to debate me on this but I'm just
sharing nearly 15 years of experience
doing this stuff when you've been caught
it's harder to get back in their good
graces for good reason they're on to you
they see what you're doing you know the
website now has a history of having been
flagged before so you now have extra
work to do for Google to trust your
website so if you choose to salvage that
s you really need to think about what
you're going to do going forward to
prove that you're not just spamming
Google again and I would wait until
you're at that point to submit any type
of reconsideration request don't waste
their time because the more you lie to
them and the more you pretend like you
fixed things and you didn't the less
likely they are to approve future
reconsideration
requests for the core update there's not
much you can do honestly when it's
rolling out if your site is going up or
down in a huge way that data has already
been processed you know it takes months
for Google to process this stuff so
doing anything reactive
deleting big chunks of your website
making huge systemic changes to your
site it's just not going to result in
any significant change in behavior
during a core update so what I would do
is keep doing things right um keep
cleaning up low performing content keep
creating high value high quality content
don't do anything major and wait for the
data to roll in so we really just do
need to be patient two or three weeks
really to see consistent data from the
core update there's going to be a lot of
people talking about the core update
between now and
then they don't necessarily know what
they're talking about either listen it's
great for all of us to share what we're
seeing right but we just need to wait
for it to be done that's really what
I've learned in my years of doing this
type of work and I think that's it so
feel free to ask me any questions and
the last thing I'll say is just be nice

stressed out everyone's like mad at

naming the name and shaming and the the
huis is just like out of control so like
Let's help each other let's support each
